rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring Case To Trial Within Five Years Pursuant To Ccp 583.310
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2024 line 1. Defendant's Motion for Failure to Bring Case to Trial within 5 years is GRANTED. Late-filed opposition is considered and stricken. It is undisputed that the matter was not brought to trial within 5 years and 6 months. It is also undisputed that the parties did not stipulate to an extension. Plaintiff presents no arguments or evidence that bringing the complaint to trial was impractical or futile. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2024 line 2. rulings.Case Title: EILEEN M. O'NEILL VS. JOHN FINCH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2024 line 3. Defendant Motion to Quash Service of Summons. Hearing Required for Defendants to provide authority that service allegedly under false pretenses/fraud (as described in the motion) is ineffective. Defendant and Domingo Balinton to be present in-person or via Zoom with video capability. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
